title: The Door at the End of the Hall
description:
Trapped in a looping hallway, you must study subtle changes to choose the right doors. Each passage resets the hall, but slightly wrong. Observe carefully, make smart choices, and uncover the path that unlocks the mysterious door at the end of the hall.

Each time you step through a door, you find yourself back at the beginning. Something always changes. An item out of place. A detail that doesn’t belong. A feeling that the hall itself is watching to see if you notice.

The Door at the End of the Hall is a first-person, 3D psychological observation/anomaly finder game where progress depends on attention, intuition, and memory. You must choose between multiple doors, using subtle environmental clues to determine which path leads you closer to escape and which ones pull you deeper into the loop.

At the far end of the hallway stands a locked door. 

To reach it, you’ll need to understand the rules of the hall, recognize what’s wrong, and learn how your choices shape each repetition. Every pass through the corridor tests your perception, rewarding careful observation, and punishing assumptions.

There is no guessing. Every change is deterministic and can be solved. The space changes when you aren’t looking—and dares you to notice.

Will you find the path that finally lets you leave?

Estimated Play TimeFor a single, complete run: ~15 - 30 minutes

Including the post-game completionist mode: ~45m - 1hr
description_short: Trapped in a looping hallway, you must study subtle changes to choose the right doors. Each passage resets the hall, but slightly wrong. Observe carefully, make smart choices, and uncover the path that unlocks the mysterious door at the end of the hall.
